name: Continuous.add
type_signature: Continuous f → Continuous g → Continuous fun x => f x + g x
docstring: The pointwise sum of two continuous functions into a topological additive group is continuous.

name: Continuous.mul
type_signature: Continuous f → Continuous g → Continuous fun x => f x * g x
docstring: The pointwise product of two continuous functions into a topological monoid is continuous.

name: Continuous.sub
type_signature: Continuous f → Continuous g → Continuous fun x => f x - g x
docstring: The pointwise difference of two continuous functions into a topological additive group is continuous.

name: Continuous.div
type_signature: Continuous f → Continuous g → (∀ x, g x ≠ 0) → Continuous fun x => f x / g x
docstring: The pointwise quotient of two continuous functions is continuous where the denominator is nonzero.

name: Continuous.neg
type_signature: Continuous f → Continuous fun x => -f x
docstring: The pointwise negation of a continuous function into a topological additive group is continuous.

name: Continuous.comp
type_signature: Continuous g → Continuous f → Continuous (g ∘ f)
docstring: Composition of continuous functions is continuous.

name: Continuous.const
type_signature: Continuous fun _ => c
docstring: A constant function is continuous.

name: continuous_id
type_signature: Continuous id
docstring: The identity function is continuous.

name: Continuous.pow
type_signature: Continuous f → Continuous fun x => f x ^ n
docstring: A natural-number power of a continuous function is continuous.

name: Continuous.inv
type_signature: Continuous f → (∀ x, f x ≠ 0) → Continuous fun x => (f x)⁻¹
docstring: The pointwise inverse of a nonvanishing continuous function is continuous.

name: ContinuousOn.add
type_signature: ContinuousOn f s → ContinuousOn g s → ContinuousOn (fun x => f x + g x) s
docstring: The pointwise sum of two functions continuous on s is continuous on s.

name: ContinuousOn.mul
type_signature: ContinuousOn f s → ContinuousOn g s → ContinuousOn (fun x => f x * g x) s
docstring: The pointwise product of two functions continuous on s is continuous on s.

name: Real.continuous_add
type_signature: Continuous fun p : ℝ × ℝ => p.1 + p.2
docstring: Addition on the reals is continuous as a function of two variables.

name: Real.continuous_mul
type_signature: Continuous fun p : ℝ × ℝ => p.1 * p.2
docstring: Multiplication on the reals is continuous as a function of two variables.

name: Real.continuous_exp
type_signature: Continuous Real.exp
docstring: The real exponential function is continuous.

name: Real.continuous_log
type_signature: ContinuousOn Real.log {x | x ≠ 0}
docstring: The real logarithm is continuous away from zero.

name: Real.continuous_sin
type_signature: Continuous Real.sin
docstring: The real sine function is continuous.

name: Real.continuous_cos
type_signature: Continuous Real.cos
docstring: The real cosine function is continuous.

name: Continuous.rpow
type_signature: Continuous f → Continuous g → (∀ x, 0 < f x ∨ g x ≠ 0) → Continuous fun x => f x ^ g x
docstring: Pointwise real power of continuous functions is continuous under a positivity or exponent condition.

name: continuous_const
type_signature: Continuous fun _ => c
docstring: Any constant function is continuous.

name: Continuous.smul
type_signature: Continuous f → Continuous g → Continuous fun x => f x • g x
docstring: Scalar multiplication of continuous functions is continuous.

name: Continuous.prod_mk
type_signature: Continuous f → Continuous g → Continuous fun x => (f x, g x)
docstring: Pairing of continuous functions is continuous.

name: IsCompact.prod
type_signature: IsCompact s → IsCompact t → IsCompact (s ×ˢ t)
docstring: The product of two compact sets is compact.

name: IsCompact.image
type_signature: IsCompact s → Continuous f → IsCompact (f '' s)
docstring: The continuous image of a compact set is compact.

name: IsCompact.union
type_signature: IsCompact s → IsCompact t → IsCompact (s ∪ t)
docstring: The union of two compact sets is compact.

name: IsCompact.inter
type_signature: IsCompact s → IsClosed t → IsCompact (s ∩ t)
docstring: The intersection of a compact set and a closed set is compact.

name: IsClosed.union
type_signature: IsClosed s → IsClosed t → IsClosed (s ∪ t)
docstring: The union of two closed sets is closed.

name: IsClosed.inter
type_signature: IsClosed s → IsClosed t → IsClosed (s ∩ t)
docstring: The intersection of two closed sets is closed.

name: IsOpen.union
type_signature: IsOpen s → IsOpen t → IsOpen (s ∪ t)
docstring: The union of two open sets is open.

name: IsOpen.inter
type_signature: IsOpen s → IsOpen t → IsOpen (s ∩ t)
docstring: The intersection of two open sets is open.

name: Filter.Tendsto.add
type_signature: Tendsto f l (𝓝 a) → Tendsto g l (𝓝 b) → Tendsto (fun x => f x + g x) l (𝓝 (a + b))
docstring: Limits commute with addition in a topological additive group.

name: Filter.Tendsto.mul
type_signature: Tendsto f l (𝓝 a) → Tendsto g l (𝓝 b) → Tendsto (fun x => f x * g x) l (𝓝 (a * b))
docstring: Limits commute with multiplication in a topological monoid.

name: Filter.Tendsto.comp
type_signature: Tendsto g m l → Tendsto f l k → Tendsto (f ∘ g) m k
docstring: Composition of limits through filters.

name: Filter.Tendsto.const
type_signature: Tendsto (fun _ => c) l (pure c)
docstring: Constant functions tend to the constant value along any filter.

name: Monotone.add
type_signature: Monotone f → Monotone g → Monotone fun x => f x + g x
docstring: The pointwise sum of two monotone functions is monotone.

name: Monotone.mul
type_signature: Monotone f → Monotone g → (∀ x, 0 ≤ f x) → (∀ x, 0 ≤ g x) → Monotone fun x => f x * g x
docstring: The pointwise product of two nonnegative monotone functions is monotone.

name: Monotone.comp
type_signature: Monotone g → Monotone f → Monotone (g ∘ f)
docstring: Composition of monotone functions is monotone.

name: Monotone.const
type_signature: Monotone fun _ => c
docstring: Constant functions are monotone.

name: StrictMono.add
type_signature: StrictMono f → Monotone g → StrictMono fun x => f x + g x
docstring: Strict plus monotone sum is strictly monotone.

name: Nat.add_comm
type_signature: ∀ (a b : ℕ), a + b = b + a
docstring: Addition on ℕ is commutative.

name: Nat.add_assoc
type_signature: ∀ (a b c : ℕ), (a + b) + c = a + (b + c)
docstring: Addition on ℕ is associative.

name: Nat.mul_comm
type_signature: ∀ (a b : ℕ), a * b = b * a
docstring: Multiplication on ℕ is commutative.

name: Real.add_comm
type_signature: ∀ (a b : ℝ), a + b = b + a
docstring: Addition on ℝ is commutative.

name: Real.mul_comm
type_signature: ∀ (a b : ℝ), a * b = b * a
docstring: Multiplication on ℝ is commutative.

name: add_comm
type_signature: ∀ {G : Type*} [inst : AddCommMonoid G] (a b : G), a + b = b + a
docstring: Addition is commutative in any additive commutative monoid.

name: mul_comm
type_signature: ∀ {G : Type*} [inst : CommMonoid G] (a b : G), a * b = b * a
docstring: Multiplication is commutative in any commutative monoid.

name: add_assoc
type_signature: ∀ {G : Type*} [inst : AddSemigroup G] (a b c : G), a + b + c = a + (b + c)
docstring: Addition is associative in any additive semigroup.

name: mul_assoc
type_signature: ∀ {G : Type*} [inst : Semigroup G] (a b c : G), a * b * c = a * (b * c)
docstring: Multiplication is associative in any semigroup.

name: Finset.sum_add_distrib
type_signature: ∑ i in s, (f i + g i) = (∑ i in s, f i) + ∑ i in s, g i
docstring: Sum distributes over pointwise addition of functions.

name: Finset.sum_comm
type_signature: ∑ x in s, ∑ y in t, f x y = ∑ y in t, ∑ x in s, f x y
docstring: Finite sums commute.

name: Real.exp_add
type_signature: ∀ (x y : ℝ), Real.exp (x + y) = Real.exp x * Real.exp y
docstring: The exponential turns sums into products.

name: Real.log_mul
type_signature: x ≠ 0 → y ≠ 0 → Real.log (x * y) = Real.log x + Real.log y
docstring: The logarithm turns nonzero products into sums.

name: deriv_add
type_signature: DifferentiableAt ℝ f x → DifferentiableAt ℝ g x → deriv (fun x => f x + g x) x = deriv f x + deriv g x
docstring: The derivative of a sum is the sum of the derivatives.

name: deriv_mul
type_signature: DifferentiableAt ℝ f x → DifferentiableAt ℝ g x → deriv (fun x => f x * g x) x = deriv f x * g x + f x * deriv g x
docstring: The product rule for derivatives.

name: Differentiable.add
type_signature: Differentiable ℝ f → Differentiable ℝ g → Differentiable ℝ fun x => f x + g x
docstring: The pointwise sum of two differentiable functions is differentiable.

name: Differentiable.mul
type_signature: Differentiable ℝ f → Differentiable ℝ g → Differentiable ℝ fun x => f x * g x
docstring: The pointwise product of two differentiable functions is differentiable.

name: Differentiable.comp
type_signature: Differentiable ℝ g → Differentiable ℝ f → Differentiable ℝ (g ∘ f)
docstring: Composition of differentiable functions is differentiable (chain rule).

name: le_trans
type_signature: a ≤ b → b ≤ c → a ≤ c
docstring: The ≤ relation is transitive.

name: lt_trans
type_signature: a < b → b < c → a < c
docstring: The < relation is transitive.

name: add_le_add
type_signature: a ≤ b → c ≤ d → a + c ≤ b + d
docstring: ≤ is preserved by pointwise addition.

name: mul_le_mul
type_signature: a ≤ b → c ≤ d → 0 ≤ c → 0 ≤ b → a * c ≤ b * d
docstring: ≤ is preserved by pointwise multiplication under nonnegativity.

name: abs_add
docstring: The triangle inequality for the absolute value.

name: isCompact_Ioc_iff

name: isCompact_Ioo_iff

name: IsCompact.exists_isMinOn
type_signature: [ClosedIicTopology α] {s : Set β} (hs : IsCompact s) (ne_s : s.Nonempty) {f : β → α} (hf : ContinuousOn f s) : ∃ x ∈ s, IsMinOn f s x
docstring: The **extreme value theorem**: a continuous function realizes its minimum on a compact set.

name: IsCompact.exists_forall_le'
type_signature: [ClosedIicTopology α] [NoMaxOrder α] {f : β → α} {s : Set β} (hs : IsCompact s) (hf : ContinuousOn f s) {a : α} (hf' : ∀ b ∈ s, a < f b) : ∃ a', a < a' ∧ ∀ b ∈ s, a' ≤ f b
docstring: If a continuous function lies strictly above `a` on a compact set, it has a lower bound strictly above `a`.

name: IsCompact.exists_isMaxOn
type_signature: [ClosedIciTopology α] {s : Set β} (hs : IsCompact s) (ne_s : s.Nonempty) {f : β → α} (hf : ContinuousOn f s) : ∃ x ∈ s, IsMaxOn f s x
docstring: The **extreme value theorem**: a continuous function realizes its maximum on a compact set.

name: ContinuousOn.exists_isMinOn'
type_signature: [ClosedIicTopology α] {s : Set β} {f : β → α} (hf : ContinuousOn f s) (hsc : IsClosed s) {x₀ : β} (h₀ : x₀ ∈ s) (hc : ∀ᶠ x in cocompact β ⊓ 𝓟 s, f x₀ ≤ f x) : ∃ x ∈ s, IsMinOn f s x
docstring: The **extreme value theorem**: if a function `f` is continuous on a closed set `s` and it is larger than a value in its image away from compact sets, then it has a minimum on this set.

name: ContinuousOn.exists_isMaxOn'
type_signature: [ClosedIciTopology α] {s : Set β} {f : β → α} (hf : ContinuousOn f s) (hsc : IsClosed s) {x₀ : β} (h₀ : x₀ ∈ s) (hc : ∀ᶠ x in cocompact β ⊓ 𝓟 s, f x ≤ f x₀) : ∃ x ∈ s, IsMaxOn f s x
docstring: The **extreme value theorem**: if a function `f` is continuous on a closed set `s` and it is smaller than a value in its image away from compact sets, then it has a maximum on this set.

name: Continuous.exists_forall_le'
type_signature: [ClosedIicTopology α] {f : β → α} (hf : Continuous f) (x₀ : β) (h : ∀ᶠ x in cocompact β, f x₀ ≤ f x) : ∃ x : β, ∀ y : β, f x ≤ f y
docstring: The **extreme value theorem**: if a continuous function `f` is larger than a value in its range away from compact sets, then it has a global minimum.

name: Continuous.exists_forall_ge'
type_signature: [ClosedIciTopology α] {f : β → α} (hf : Continuous f) (x₀ : β) (h : ∀ᶠ x in cocompact β, f x ≤ f x₀) : ∃ x : β, ∀ y : β, f y ≤ f x
docstring: The **extreme value theorem**: if a continuous function `f` is smaller than a value in its range away from compact sets, then it has a global maximum.

name: Continuous.exists_forall_le
type_signature: [ClosedIicTopology α] [Nonempty β] {f : β → α} (hf : Continuous f) (hlim : Tendsto f (cocompact β) atTop) : ∃ x, ∀ y, f x ≤ f y
docstring: The **extreme value theorem**: if a continuous function `f` tends to infinity away from compact sets, then it has a global minimum.

name: Continuous.exists_forall_ge
type_signature: [ClosedIciTopology α] [Nonempty β] {f : β → α} (hf : Continuous f) (hlim : Tendsto f (cocompact β) atBot) : ∃ x, ∀ y, f y ≤ f x
docstring: The **extreme value theorem**: if a continuous function `f` tends to negative infinity away from compact sets, then it has a global maximum.

name: Continuous.exists_forall_le_of_hasCompactMulSupport

docstring: A continuous function with compact support has a global minimum.

name: Continuous.exists_forall_ge_of_hasCompactMulSupport

docstring: A continuous function with compact support has a global maximum.

name: IsCompact.exists_clusterPt
type_signature: (hs : IsCompact s) {f : Filter X} [NeBot f] (hf : f ≤ 𝓟 s) : ∃ x ∈ s, ClusterPt x f

name: IsCompact.exists_mapClusterPt
type_signature: {ι : Type*} (hs : IsCompact s) {f : Filter ι} [NeBot f] {u : ι → X} (hf : Filter.map u f ≤ 𝓟 s) : ∃ x ∈ s, MapClusterPt x f u

name: IsCompact.exists_clusterPt_of_frequently
type_signature: {l : Filter X} (hs : IsCompact s) (hl : ∃ᶠ x in l, x ∈ s) : ∃ a ∈ s, ClusterPt a l

name: IsCompact.exists_mapClusterPt_of_frequently
type_signature: {l : Filter ι} {f : ι → X} (hs : IsCompact s) (hf : ∃ᶠ x in l, f x ∈ s) : ∃ a ∈ s, MapClusterPt a l f

name: IsCompact.compl_mem_sets
type_signature: (hs : IsCompact s) {f : Filter X} (hf : ∀ x ∈ s, sᶜ ∈ 𝓝 x ⊓ f) : sᶜ ∈ f
docstring: The complement to a compact set belongs to a filter `f` if it belongs to each filter `𝓝 x ⊓ f`, `x ∈ s`.

name: IsCompact.compl_mem_sets_of_nhdsWithin
type_signature: (hs : IsCompact s) {f : Filter X} (hf : ∀ x ∈ s, ∃ t ∈ 𝓝[s] x, tᶜ ∈ f) : sᶜ ∈ f
docstring: The complement to a compact set belongs to a filter `f` if each `x ∈ s` has a neighborhood `t` within `s` such that `tᶜ` belongs to `f`.

name: IsCompact.induction_on
type_signature: (hs : IsCompact s) {p : Set X → Prop} (he : p ∅) (hmono : ∀ ⦃s t⦄, s ⊆ t → p t → p s) (hunion : ∀ ⦃s t⦄, p s → p t → p (s ∪ t)) (hnhds : ∀ x ∈ s, ∃ t ∈ 𝓝[s] x, p t) : p s
docstring: If `p : Set X → Prop` is stable under restriction and union, and each point `x` of a compact set `s` has a neighborhood `t` within `s` such that `p t`, then `p s` holds.

name: IsCompact.inter_right
type_signature: (hs : IsCompact s) (ht : IsClosed t) : IsCompact (s ∩ t)
docstring: The intersection of a compact set and a closed set is a compact set.

name: IsCompact.inter_left
type_signature: (ht : IsCompact t) (hs : IsClosed s) : IsCompact (s ∩ t)
docstring: The intersection of a closed set and a compact set is a compact set.

name: IsCompact.diff
type_signature: (hs : IsCompact s) (ht : IsOpen t) : IsCompact (s \ t)
docstring: The set difference of a compact set and an open set is a compact set.

name: IsCompact.of_isClosed_subset
type_signature: (hs : IsCompact s) (ht : IsClosed t) (h : t ⊆ s) : IsCompact t
docstring: A closed subset of a compact set is a compact set.

name: IsCompact.image_of_continuousOn
type_signature: {f : X → Y} (hs : IsCompact s) (hf : ContinuousOn f s) : IsCompact (f '' s)

name: IsCompact.adherence_nhdset
type_signature: {f : Filter X} (hs : IsCompact s) (hf₂ : f ≤ 𝓟 s) (ht₁ : IsOpen t) (ht₂ : ∀ x ∈ s, ClusterPt x f → x ∈ t) : t ∈ f

name: isCompact_iff_ultrafilter_le_nhds
type_signature: : IsCompact s ↔ ∀ f : Ultrafilter X, ↑f ≤ 𝓟 s → ∃ x ∈ s, ↑f ≤ 𝓝 x

name: isCompact_iff_ultrafilter_le_nhds'
type_signature: : IsCompact s ↔ ∀ f : Ultrafilter X, s ∈ f → ∃ x ∈ s, ↑f ≤ 𝓝 x

name: IsCompact.le_nhds_of_unique_clusterPt
type_signature: (hs : IsCompact s) {l : Filter X} {y : X} (hmem : s ∈ l) (h : ∀ x ∈ s, ClusterPt x l → x = y) : l ≤ 𝓝 y
docstring: If a compact set belongs to a filter and this filter has a unique cluster point `y` in this set, then the filter is less than or equal to `𝓝 y`.

name: IsCompact.tendsto_nhds_of_unique_mapClusterPt
type_signature: {Y} {l : Filter Y} {y : X} {f : Y → X} (hs : IsCompact s) (hmem : ∀ᶠ x in l, f x ∈ s) (h : ∀ x ∈ s, MapClusterPt x l f → x = y) : Tendsto f l (𝓝 y)
docstring: If values of `f : Y → X` belong to a compact set `s` eventually along a filter `l` and `y` is a unique `MapClusterPt` for `f` along `l` in `s`, then `f` tends to `𝓝 y` along `l`.

name: IsCompact.elim_directed_cover
type_signature: {ι : Type v} [hι : Nonempty ι] (hs : IsCompact s) (U : ι → Set X) (hUo : ∀ i, IsOpen (U i)) (hsU : s ⊆ ⋃ i, U i) (hdU : Directed (· ⊆ ·) U) : ∃ i, s ⊆ U i
docstring: For every open directed cover of a compact set, there exists a single element of the cover which itself includes the set.

name: IsCompact.elim_finite_subcover
type_signature: {ι : Type v} (hs : IsCompact s) (U : ι → Set X) (hUo : ∀ i, IsOpen (U i)) (hsU : s ⊆ ⋃ i, U i) : ∃ t : Finset ι, s ⊆ ⋃ i ∈ t, U i
docstring: For every open cover of a compact set, there exists a finite subcover.

name: IsCompact.elim_nhds_subcover_nhdsSet'
type_signature: (hs : IsCompact s) (U : ∀ x ∈ s, Set X) (hU : ∀ x hx, U x hx ∈ 𝓝 x) : ∃ t : Finset s, (⋃ x ∈ t, U x.1 x.2) ∈ 𝓝ˢ s

name: IsCompact.elim_nhds_subcover_nhdsSet
type_signature: (hs : IsCompact s) {U : X → Set X} (hU : ∀ x ∈ s, U x ∈ 𝓝 x) : ∃ t : Finset X, (∀ x ∈ t, x ∈ s) ∧ (⋃ x ∈ t, U x) ∈ 𝓝ˢ s

name: IsCompact.elim_nhds_subcover'
type_signature: (hs : IsCompact s) (U : ∀ x ∈ s, Set X) (hU : ∀ x (hx : x ∈ s), U x ‹x ∈ s› ∈ 𝓝 x) : ∃ t : Finset s, s ⊆ ⋃ x ∈ t, U (x : s) x.2

name: IsCompact.elim_nhds_subcover
type_signature: (hs : IsCompact s) (U : X → Set X) (hU : ∀ x ∈ s, U x ∈ 𝓝 x) : ∃ t : Finset X, (∀ x ∈ t, x ∈ s) ∧ s ⊆ ⋃ x ∈ t, U x

name: IsCompact.elim_nhdsWithin_subcover'
type_signature: (hs : IsCompact s) (U : ∀ x ∈ s, Set X) (hU : ∀ x (hx : x ∈ s), U x hx ∈ 𝓝[s] x) : ∃ t : Finset s, s ⊆ ⋃ x ∈ t, U x x.2

name: IsCompact.elim_nhdsWithin_subcover
type_signature: (hs : IsCompact s) (U : X → Set X) (hU : ∀ x ∈ s, U x ∈ 𝓝[s] x) : ∃ t : Finset X, (∀ x ∈ t, x ∈ s) ∧ s ⊆ ⋃ x ∈ t, U x

name: IsCompact.disjoint_nhdsSet_left
type_signature: {l : Filter X} (hs : IsCompact s) : Disjoint (𝓝ˢ s) l ↔ ∀ x ∈ s, Disjoint (𝓝 x) l
docstring: The neighborhood filter of a compact set is disjoint with a filter `l` if and only if the neighborhood filter of each point of this set is disjoint with `l`.

name: IsCompact.disjoint_nhdsSet_right
type_signature: {l : Filter X} (hs : IsCompact s) : Disjoint l (𝓝ˢ s) ↔ ∀ x ∈ s, Disjoint l (𝓝 x)
docstring: A filter `l` is disjoint with the neighborhood filter of a compact set if and only if it is disjoint with the neighborhood filter of each point of this set.

name: IsCompact.elim_directed_family_closed
type_signature: {ι : Type v} [Nonempty ι] (hs : IsCompact s) (t : ι → Set X) (htc : ∀ i, IsClosed (t i)) (hst : (s ∩ ⋂ i, t i) = ∅) (hdt : Directed (· ⊇ ·) t) : ∃ i : ι, s ∩ t i = ∅
docstring: For every directed family of closed sets whose intersection avoids a compact set, there exists a single element of the family which itself avoids this compact set.

name: IsCompact.elim_finite_subfamily_closed
type_signature: {ι : Type v} (hs : IsCompact s) (t : ι → Set X) (htc : ∀ i, IsClosed (t i)) (hst : (s ∩ ⋂ i, t i) = ∅) : ∃ u : Finset ι, (s ∩ ⋂ i ∈ u, t i) = ∅
docstring: For every family of closed sets whose intersection avoids a compact set, there exists a finite subfamily whose intersection avoids this compact set.

name: IsCompact.inter_iInter_nonempty
type_signature: {ι : Type v} (hs : IsCompact s) (t : ι → Set X) (htc : ∀ i, IsClosed (t i)) (hst : ∀ u : Finset ι, (s ∩ ⋂ i ∈ u, t i).Nonempty) : (s ∩ ⋂ i, t i).Nonempty
docstring: To show that a compact set intersects the intersection of a family of closed sets, it is sufficient to show that it intersects every finite subfamily.

name: IsCompact.nonempty_iInter_of_directed_nonempty_isCompact_isClosed
type_signature: {ι : Type v} [hι : Nonempty ι] (t : ι → Set X) (htd : Directed (· ⊇ ·) t) (htn : ∀ i, (t i).Nonempty) (htc : ∀ i, IsCompact (t i)) (htcl : ∀ i, IsClosed (t i)) : (⋂ i, t i).Nonempty
docstring: Cantor's intersection theorem for `iInter`: the intersection of a directed family of nonempty compact closed sets is nonempty.

name: IsCompact.nonempty_sInter_of_directed_nonempty_isCompact_isClosed
type_signature: {S : Set (Set X)} [hS : Nonempty S] (hSd : DirectedOn (· ⊇ ·) S) (hSn : ∀ U ∈ S, U.Nonempty) (hSc : ∀ U ∈ S, IsCompact U) (hScl : ∀ U ∈ S, IsClosed U) : (⋂₀ S).Nonempty
docstring: Cantor's intersection theorem for `sInter`: the intersection of a directed family of nonempty compact closed sets is nonempty.

name: IsCompact.nonempty_iInter_of_sequence_nonempty_isCompact_isClosed
type_signature: (t : ℕ → Set X) (htd : ∀ i, t (i + 1) ⊆ t i) (htn : ∀ i, (t i).Nonempty) (ht0 : IsCompact (t 0)) (htcl : ∀ i, IsClosed (t i)) : (⋂ i, t i).Nonempty
docstring: Cantor's intersection theorem for sequences indexed by `ℕ`: the intersection of a decreasing sequence of nonempty compact closed sets is nonempty.

name: IsCompact.elim_finite_subcover_image
type_signature: {b : Set ι} {c : ι → Set X} (hs : IsCompact s) (hc₁ : ∀ i ∈ b, IsOpen (c i)) (hc₂ : s ⊆ ⋃ i ∈ b, c i) : ∃ b', b' ⊆ b ∧ Set.Finite b' ∧ s ⊆ ⋃ i ∈ b', c i
docstring: For every open cover of a compact set, there exists a finite subcover.

name: isCompact_of_finite_subcover
type_signature: (h : ∀ {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Set X), (∀ i, IsOpen (U i)) → (s ⊆ ⋃ i, U i) → ∃ t : Finset ι, s ⊆ ⋃ i ∈ t, U i) : IsCompact s
docstring: A set `s` is compact if for every open cover of `s`, there exists a finite subcover.

name: isCompact_of_finite_subfamily_closed
type_signature: (h : ∀ {ι : Type u} (t : ι → Set X), (∀ i, IsClosed (t i)) → (s ∩ ⋂ i, t i) = ∅ → ∃ u : Finset ι, (s ∩ ⋂ i ∈ u, t i) = ∅) : IsCompact s
docstring: A set `s` is compact if for every family of closed sets whose intersection avoids `s`, there exists a finite subfamily whose intersection avoids `s`.

name: isCompact_iff_finite_subcover
type_signature: : IsCompact s ↔ ∀ {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Set X), (∀ i, IsOpen (U i)) → (s ⊆ ⋃ i, U i) → ∃ t : Finset ι, s ⊆ ⋃ i ∈ t, U i
docstring: A set `s` is compact if and only if for every open cover of `s`, there exists a finite subcover.

name: isCompact_iff_finite_subfamily_closed
type_signature: : IsCompact s ↔ ∀ {ι : Type u} (t : ι → Set X), (∀ i, IsClosed (t i)) → (s ∩ ⋂ i, t i) = ∅ → ∃ u : Finset ι, (s ∩ ⋂ i ∈ u, t i) = ∅
docstring: A set `s` is compact if and only if for every family of closed sets whose intersection avoids `s`, there exists a finite subfamily whose intersection avoids `s`.

name: IsCompact.mem_nhdsSet_prod_of_forall
type_signature: {K : Set X} {Y} {l : Filter Y} {s : Set (X × Y)} (hK : IsCompact K) (hs : ∀ x ∈ K, s ∈ 𝓝 x ×ˢ l) : s ∈ (𝓝ˢ K) ×ˢ l
docstring: If `s : Set (X × Y)` belongs to `𝓝 x ×ˢ l` for all `x` from a compact set `K`, then it belongs to `(𝓝ˢ K) ×ˢ l`, i.e., there exist an open `U ⊇ K` and `t ∈ l` such that `U ×ˢ t ⊆ s`.

name: IsCompact.nhdsSet_prod_eq_biSup
type_signature: {K : Set X} (hK : IsCompact K) {Y} (l : Filter Y) : (𝓝ˢ K) ×ˢ l = ⨆ x ∈ K, 𝓝 x ×ˢ l

name: IsCompact.prod_nhdsSet_eq_biSup
type_signature: {K : Set Y} (hK : IsCompact K) {X} (l : Filter X) : l ×ˢ (𝓝ˢ K) = ⨆ y ∈ K, l ×ˢ 𝓝 y

name: IsCompact.mem_prod_nhdsSet_of_forall
type_signature: {K : Set Y} {X} {l : Filter X} {s : Set (X × Y)} (hK : IsCompact K) (hs : ∀ y ∈ K, s ∈ l ×ˢ 𝓝 y) : s ∈ l ×ˢ 𝓝ˢ K
docstring: If `s : Set (X × Y)` belongs to `l ×ˢ 𝓝 y` for all `y` from a compact set `K`, then it belongs to `l ×ˢ (𝓝ˢ K)`, i.e., there exist `t ∈ l` and an open `U ⊇ K` such that `t ×ˢ U ⊆ s`.

name: IsCompact.nhdsSet_inf_eq_biSup
type_signature: {K : Set X} (hK : IsCompact K) (l : Filter X) : (𝓝ˢ K) ⊓ l = ⨆ x ∈ K, 𝓝 x ⊓ l

name: IsCompact.inf_nhdsSet_eq_biSup
type_signature: {K : Set X} (hK : IsCompact K) (l : Filter X) : l ⊓ (𝓝ˢ K) = ⨆ x ∈ K, l ⊓ 𝓝 x

name: IsCompact.mem_nhdsSet_inf_of_forall
type_signature: {K : Set X} {l : Filter X} {s : Set X} (hK : IsCompact K) (hs : ∀ x ∈ K, s ∈ 𝓝 x ⊓ l) : s ∈ (𝓝ˢ K) ⊓ l
docstring: If `s : Set X` belongs to `𝓝 x ⊓ l` for all `x` from a compact set `K`, then it belongs to `(𝓝ˢ K) ⊓ l`, i.e., there exist an open `U ⊇ K` and `T ∈ l` such that `U ∩ T ⊆ s`.

name: IsCompact.mem_inf_nhdsSet_of_forall
type_signature: {K : Set X} {l : Filter X} {s : Set X} (hK : IsCompact K) (hs : ∀ y ∈ K, s ∈ l ⊓ 𝓝 y) : s ∈ l ⊓ 𝓝ˢ K
docstring: If `s : Set S` belongs to `l ⊓ 𝓝 x` for all `x` from a compact set `K`, then it belongs to `l ⊓ (𝓝ˢ K)`, i.e., there exist `T ∈ l` and an open `U ⊇ K` such that `T ∩ U ⊆ s`.

name: IsCompact.eventually_forall_of_forall_eventually
type_signature: {x₀ : X} {K : Set Y} (hK : IsCompact K) {P : X → Y → Prop} (hP : ∀ y ∈ K, ∀ᶠ z : X × Y in 𝓝 (x₀, y), P z.1 z.2) : ∀ᶠ x in 𝓝 x₀, ∀ y ∈ K, P x y
docstring: To show that `∀ y ∈ K, P x y` holds for `x` close enough to `x₀` when `K` is compact, it is sufficient to show that for all `y₀ ∈ K` there `P x y` holds for `(x, y)` close enough to `(x₀, y₀)`. Provided for backwards compatibility, see `IsCompact.mem_prod_nhdsSet_of_forall` for a stronger statement.

docstring: If `s` and `t` are compact sets and `n` is an open neighborhood of `s × t`, then there exist open neighborhoods `u ⊇ s` and `v ⊇ t` such that `u × v ⊆ n`. See also `IsCompact.nhdsSet_prod_eq`.

docstring: A relative version of `IsCompact.nhdsSet_prod_eq`: if `s` and `t` are compact sets, then the neighborhoods filter of `s ×ˢ t` within `s' ×ˢ t'` is the product of the neighborhoods filters of `s` and `t` within `s'` and `t'`. For general sets, only the `≤` inequality holds, see `nhdsSetWithin_prod_le`.

name: Metric.complete_of_convergent_controlled_sequences
type_signature: (B : ℕ → Real) (hB : ∀ n, 0 < B n) (H : ∀ u : ℕ → α, (∀ N n m : ℕ, N ≤ n → N ≤ m → dist (u n) (u m) < B N) → ∃ x, Tendsto u atTop (𝓝 x)) : CompleteSpace α
docstring: A very useful criterion to show that a space is complete is to show that all sequences which satisfy a bound of the form `dist (u n) (u m) < B N` for all `n m ≥ N` are converging. This is often applied for `B N = 2^{-N}`, i.e., with a very fast convergence to `0`, which makes it possible to use arguments of converging series, while this is impossible to do in general for arbitrary Cauchy sequences.

docstring: If the distance between `s n` and `s m`, `n ≤ m` is bounded above by `b n` and `b` converges to zero, then `s` is a Cauchy sequence.

docstring: If a globally contracting map `f` has two complete forward-invariant sets `s`, `t`, and `x ∈ s` is at a finite distance from `y ∈ t`, then the `efixedPoint'` constructed by `x` is the same as the `efixedPoint'` constructed by `y`. This lemma takes additional arguments stating that `f` contracts on `s` and `t` because this way it can be used to prove the desired equality with non-trivial proofs of these facts.

name: limsSup
type_signature: (f : Filter α) : α
docstring: The `limsSup` of a filter `f` is the infimum of the `a` such that the inequality `x ≤ a` eventually holds for `f`.

name: limsInf
type_signature: (f : Filter α) : α
docstring: The `limsInf` of a filter `f` is the supremum of the `a` such that the inequality `x ≥ a` eventually holds for `f`.

name: limsup
type_signature: (u : β → α) (f : Filter β) : α
docstring: The `limsup` of a function `u` along a filter `f` is the infimum of the `a` such that the inequality `u x ≤ a` eventually holds for `f`.

name: liminf
type_signature: (u : β → α) (f : Filter β) : α
docstring: The `liminf` of a function `u` along a filter `f` is the supremum of the `a` such that the inequality `u x ≥ a` eventually holds for `f`.

name: blimsup
type_signature: (u : β → α) (f : Filter β) (p : β → Prop)
docstring: The `blimsup` of a function `u` along a filter `f`, bounded by a predicate `p`, is the infimum of the `a` such that the inequality `u x ≤ a` eventually holds for `f`, whenever `p x` holds.

name: bliminf
type_signature: (u : β → α) (f : Filter β) (p : β → Prop)
docstring: The `bliminf` of a function `u` along a filter `f`, bounded by a predicate `p`, is the supremum of the `a` such that the inequality `a ≤ u x` eventually holds for `f` whenever `p x` holds.

name: limsup_eq

name: liminf_eq

name: blimsup_eq
type_signature: : blimsup u f p = sInf { a | ∀ᶠ x in f, p x → u x ≤ a }

name: bliminf_eq
type_signature: : bliminf u f p = sSup { a | ∀ᶠ x in f, p x → a ≤ u x }

name: liminf_reparam
type_signature: noncomputable def liminf_reparam (f : ι → α) (s : ι' → Set ι) (p : ι' → Prop) [Countable (Subtype p)] [Nonempty (Subtype p)] (j : Subtype p) : Subtype p
docstring: Given an indexed family of sets `s j` over `j : Subtype p` and a function `f`, then `liminf_reparam j` is equal to `j` if `f` is bounded below on `s j`, and otherwise to some index `k` such that `f` is bounded below on `s k` (if there exists one). To ensure good measurability behavior, this index `k` is chosen as the minimal suitable index. This function is used to write down a liminf in a measurable way, in `Filter.HasBasis.liminf_eq_ciSup_ciInf` and `Filter.HasBasis.liminf_eq_ite`.

name: HasBasis.liminf_eq_ciSup_ciInf
type_signature: {v : Filter ι} {p : ι' → Prop} {s : ι' → Set ι} [Countable (Subtype p)] [Nonempty (Subtype p)] (hv : v.HasBasis p s) {f : ι → α} (hs : ∀ (j : Subtype p), (s j).Nonempty) (H : ∃ (j : Subtype p), BddBelow (range (fun (i : s j) ↦ f i))) : liminf f v = ⨆ (j : Subtype p), ⨅ (i : s (liminf_reparam f s p j)), f i
docstring: Writing a liminf as a supremum of infimum, in a (possibly non-complete) conditionally complete linear order. A reparametrization trick is needed to avoid taking the infimum of sets which are not bounded below.

name: HasBasis.liminf_eq_ite
type_signature: {v : Filter ι} {p : ι' → Prop} {s : ι' → Set ι} [Countable (Subtype p)] [Nonempty (Subtype p)] (hv : v.HasBasis p s) (f : ι → α) : liminf f v = if ∃ (j : Subtype p), s j = ∅ then sSup univ else if ∀ (j : Subtype p), ¬BddBelow (range (fun (i : s j) ↦ f i)) then sSup ∅ else ⨆ (j : Subtype p), ⨅ (i : s (liminf_reparam f s p j)), f i
docstring: Writing a liminf as a supremum of infimum, in a (possibly non-complete) conditionally complete linear order. A reparametrization trick is needed to avoid taking the infimum of sets which are not bounded below.

name: limsup_reparam
type_signature: noncomputable def limsup_reparam (f : ι → α) (s : ι' → Set ι) (p : ι' → Prop) [Countable (Subtype p)] [Nonempty (Subtype p)] (j : Subtype p) : Subtype p
docstring: Given an indexed family of sets `s j` and a function `f`, then `limsup_reparam j` is equal to `j` if `f` is bounded above on `s j`, and otherwise to some index `k` such that `f` is bounded above on `s k` (if there exists one). To ensure good measurability behavior, this index `k` is chosen as the minimal suitable index. This function is used to write down a limsup in a measurable way, in `Filter.HasBasis.limsup_eq_ciInf_ciSup` and `Filter.HasBasis.limsup_eq_ite`.

name: HasBasis.limsup_eq_ciInf_ciSup
type_signature: {v : Filter ι} {p : ι' → Prop} {s : ι' → Set ι} [Countable (Subtype p)] [Nonempty (Subtype p)] (hv : v.HasBasis p s) {f : ι → α} (hs : ∀ (j : Subtype p), (s j).Nonempty) (H : ∃ (j : Subtype p), BddAbove (range (fun (i : s j) ↦ f i))) : limsup f v = ⨅ (j : Subtype p), ⨆ (i : s (limsup_reparam f s p j)), f i
docstring: Writing a limsup as an infimum of supremum, in a (possibly non-complete) conditionally complete linear order. A reparametrization trick is needed to avoid taking the supremum of sets which are not bounded above.

name: HasBasis.limsup_eq_ite

name: image_le_of_liminf_slope_right_lt_deriv_boundary'
type_signature: {f f' : ℝ → ℝ} {a b : ℝ} (hf : ContinuousOn f (Icc a b)) -- `hf'` actually says `liminf (f z - f x) / (z - x) ≤ f' x` (hf' : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, ∀ r, f' x < r → ∃ᶠ z in 𝓝[>] x, slope f x z < r) {B B' : ℝ → ℝ} (ha : f a ≤ B a) (hB : ContinuousOn B (Icc a b)) (hB' : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, HasDerivWithinAt B (B' x) (Ici x) x) (bound : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, f x = B x → f' x < B' x) : ∀ ⦃x⦄, x ∈ Icc a b → f x ≤ B x
docstring: General fencing theorem for continuous functions with an estimate on the derivative. Let `f` and `B` be continuous functions on `[a, b]` such that * `f a ≤ B a`; * `B` has right derivative `B'` at every point of `[a, b)`; * for each `x ∈ [a, b)` the right-side limit inferior of `(f z - f x) / (z - x)` is bounded above by a function `f'`; * we have `f' x < B' x` whenever `f x = B x`. Then `f x ≤ B x` everywhere on `[a, b]`.

name: image_le_of_liminf_slope_right_lt_deriv_boundary
type_signature: {f f' : ℝ → ℝ} {a b : ℝ} (hf : ContinuousOn f (Icc a b)) -- `hf'` actually says `liminf (f z - f x) / (z - x) ≤ f' x` (hf' : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, ∀ r, f' x < r → ∃ᶠ z in 𝓝[>] x, slope f x z < r) {B B' : ℝ → ℝ} (ha : f a ≤ B a) (hB : ∀ x, HasDerivAt B (B' x) x) (bound : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, f x = B x → f' x < B' x) : ∀ ⦃x⦄, x ∈ Icc a b → f x ≤ B x
docstring: General fencing theorem for continuous functions with an estimate on the derivative. Let `f` and `B` be continuous functions on `[a, b]` such that * `f a ≤ B a`; * `B` has derivative `B'` everywhere on `ℝ`; * for each `x ∈ [a, b)` the right-side limit inferior of `(f z - f x) / (z - x)` is bounded above by a function `f'`; * we have `f' x < B' x` whenever `f x = B x`. Then `f x ≤ B x` everywhere on `[a, b]`.

name: image_le_of_liminf_slope_right_le_deriv_boundary
type_signature: {f : ℝ → ℝ} {a b : ℝ} (hf : ContinuousOn f (Icc a b)) {B B' : ℝ → ℝ} (ha : f a ≤ B a) (hB : ContinuousOn B (Icc a b)) (hB' : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, HasDerivWithinAt B (B' x) (Ici x) x) -- `bound` actually says `liminf (f z - f x) / (z - x) ≤ B' x` (bound : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, ∀ r, B' x < r → ∃ᶠ z in 𝓝[>] x, slope f x z < r) : ∀ ⦃x⦄, x ∈ Icc a b → f x ≤ B x
docstring: General fencing theorem for continuous functions with an estimate on the derivative. Let `f` and `B` be continuous functions on `[a, b]` such that * `f a ≤ B a`; * `B` has right derivative `B'` at every point of `[a, b)`; * for each `x ∈ [a, b)` the right-side limit inferior of `(f z - f x) / (z - x)` is bounded above by `B'`. Then `f x ≤ B x` everywhere on `[a, b]`.

name: image_le_of_deriv_right_lt_deriv_boundary'
type_signature: {f f' : ℝ → ℝ} {a b : ℝ} (hf : ContinuousOn f (Icc a b)) (hf' : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, HasDerivWithinAt f (f' x) (Ici x) x) {B B' : ℝ → ℝ} (ha : f a ≤ B a) (hB : ContinuousOn B (Icc a b)) (hB' : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, HasDerivWithinAt B (B' x) (Ici x) x) (bound : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, f x = B x → f' x < B' x) : ∀ ⦃x⦄, x ∈ Icc a b → f x ≤ B x
docstring: General fencing theorem for continuous functions with an estimate on the derivative. Let `f` and `B` be continuous functions on `[a, b]` such that * `f a ≤ B a`; * `B` has right derivative `B'` at every point of `[a, b)`; * `f` has right derivative `f'` at every point of `[a, b)`; * we have `f' x < B' x` whenever `f x = B x`. Then `f x ≤ B x` everywhere on `[a, b]`.

name: image_le_of_deriv_right_lt_deriv_boundary
type_signature: {f f' : ℝ → ℝ} {a b : ℝ} (hf : ContinuousOn f (Icc a b)) (hf' : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, HasDerivWithinAt f (f' x) (Ici x) x) {B B' : ℝ → ℝ} (ha : f a ≤ B a) (hB : ∀ x, HasDerivAt B (B' x) x) (bound : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, f x = B x → f' x < B' x) : ∀ ⦃x⦄, x ∈ Icc a b → f x ≤ B x
docstring: General fencing theorem for continuous functions with an estimate on the derivative. Let `f` and `B` be continuous functions on `[a, b]` such that * `f a ≤ B a`; * `B` has derivative `B'` everywhere on `ℝ`; * `f` has right derivative `f'` at every point of `[a, b)`; * we have `f' x < B' x` whenever `f x = B x`. Then `f x ≤ B x` everywhere on `[a, b]`.

name: image_le_of_deriv_right_le_deriv_boundary
type_signature: {f f' : ℝ → ℝ} {a b : ℝ} (hf : ContinuousOn f (Icc a b)) (hf' : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, HasDerivWithinAt f (f' x) (Ici x) x) {B B' : ℝ → ℝ} (ha : f a ≤ B a) (hB : ContinuousOn B (Icc a b)) (hB' : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, HasDerivWithinAt B (B' x) (Ici x) x) (bound : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, f' x ≤ B' x) : ∀ ⦃x⦄, x ∈ Icc a b → f x ≤ B x
docstring: General fencing theorem for continuous functions with an estimate on the derivative. Then `f x ≤ B x` everywhere on `[a, b]`.

name: image_norm_le_of_liminf_right_slope_norm_lt_deriv_boundary
type_signature: {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] {f : ℝ → E} {f' : ℝ → ℝ} (hf : ContinuousOn f (Icc a b)) -- `hf'` actually says `liminf (‖f z‖ - ‖f x‖) / (z - x) ≤ f' x` (hf' : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, ∀ r, f' x < r → ∃ᶠ z in 𝓝[>] x, slope (norm ∘ f) x z < r) {B B' : ℝ → ℝ} (ha : ‖f a‖ ≤ B a) (hB : ContinuousOn B (Icc a b)) (hB' : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, HasDerivWithinAt B (B' x) (Ici x) x) (bound : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, ‖f x‖ = B x → f' x < B' x) : ∀ ⦃x⦄, x ∈ Icc a b → ‖f x‖ ≤ B x
docstring: General fencing theorem for continuous functions with an estimate on the derivative. Let `f` and `B` be continuous functions on `[a, b]` such that * `‖f a‖ ≤ B a`; * `B` has right derivative at every point of `[a, b)`; * for each `x ∈ [a, b)` the right-side limit inferior of `(‖f z‖ - ‖f x‖) / (z - x)` is bounded above by a function `f'`; * we have `f' x < B' x` whenever `‖f x‖ = B x`. Then `‖f x‖ ≤ B x` everywhere on `[a, b]`.

name: image_norm_le_of_norm_deriv_right_lt_deriv_boundary'
type_signature: {f' : ℝ → E} (hf : ContinuousOn f (Icc a b)) (hf' : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, HasDerivWithinAt f (f' x) (Ici x) x) {B B' : ℝ → ℝ} (ha : ‖f a‖ ≤ B a) (hB : ContinuousOn B (Icc a b)) (hB' : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, HasDerivWithinAt B (B' x) (Ici x) x) (bound : ∀ x ∈ Ico a b, ‖f x‖ = B x → ‖f' x‖ < B' x) : ∀ ⦃x⦄, x ∈ Icc a b → ‖f x‖ ≤ B x
docstring: General fencing theorem for continuous functions with an estimate on the norm of the derivative. Let `f` and `B` be continuous functions on `[a, b]` such that * `‖f a‖ ≤ B a`; * `f` and `B` have right derivatives `f'` and `B'` respectively at every point of `[a, b)`; * the norm of `f'` is strictly less than `B'` whenever `‖f x‖ = B x`. Then `‖f x‖ ≤ B x` everywhere on `[a, b]`. We use one-sided derivatives in the assumptions to make this theorem work for piecewise differentiable functions.

docstring: General fencing theorem for continuous functions with an estimate on the norm of the derivative. Then `‖f x‖ ≤ B x` everywhere on `[a, b]`. We use one-sided derivatives in the assumptions to make this theorem work for piecewise differentiable functions.

name: exists_nhdsWithin_lipschitzOnWith_of_hasFDerivWithinAt_of_nnnorm_lt
type_signature: (hs : Convex ℝ s) {f : E → G} (hder : ∀ᶠ y in 𝓝[s] x, HasFDerivWithinAt f (f' y) s y) (hcont : ContinuousWithinAt f' s x) (K : ℝ≥0) (hK : ‖f' x‖₊ < K) : ∃ t ∈ 𝓝[s] x, LipschitzOnWith K f t
docstring: Let `s` be a convex set in a real normed vector space `E`, let `f : E → G` be a function differentiable within `s` in a neighborhood of `x : E` with derivative `f'`. Suppose that `f'` is continuous within `s` at `x`. Then for any number `K : ℝ≥0` larger than `‖f' x‖₊`, `f` is `K`-Lipschitz on some neighborhood of `x` within `s`. See also `Convex.exists_nhdsWithin_lipschitzOnWith_of_hasFDerivWithinAt` for a version that claims existence of `K` instead of an explicit estimate.

docstring: For any positive `ε`, define on an encodable type a positive sequence with sum less than `ε`

name: Set.Countable.exists_pos_hasSum_le
type_signature: {ι : Type*} {s : Set ι} (hs : s.Countable) {ε : ℝ} (hε : 0 < ε) : ∃ ε' : ι → ℝ, (∀ i, 0 < ε' i) ∧ ∃ c, HasSum (fun i : s ↦ ε' i) c ∧ c ≤ ε

name: Set.Countable.exists_pos_forall_sum_le
type_signature: {ι : Type*} {s : Set ι} (hs : s.Countable) {ε : ℝ} (hε : 0 < ε) : ∃ ε' : ι → ℝ, (∀ i, 0 < ε' i) ∧ ∀ t : Finset ι, ↑t ⊆ s → ∑ i ∈ t, ε' i ≤ ε

name: exists_pos_sum_of_countable
type_signature: {ε : ℝ≥0} (hε : ε ≠ 0) (ι) [Countable ι] : ∃ ε' : ι → ℝ≥0, (∀ i, 0 < ε' i) ∧ ∃ c, HasSum ε' c ∧ c < ε

name: exists_pos_sum_of_countable'
type_signature: {ε : ℝ≥0∞} (hε : ε ≠ 0) (ι) [Countable ι] : ∃ ε' : ι → ℝ≥0∞, (∀ i, 0 < ε' i) ∧ ∑' i, ε' i < ε

type_signature: : Tendsto Nat.factorial atTop atTop

name: tendsto_factorial_div_pow_self_atTop
type_signature: : Tendsto (fun n ↦ n ! / (n : ℝ) ^ n : ℕ → ℝ) atTop (𝓝 0)

name: tendsto_nat_floor_atTop
type_signature: {α : Type*} [Semiring α] [LinearOrder α] [IsStrictOrderedRing α] [FloorSemiring α] : Tendsto (fun x : α ↦ ⌊x⌋₊) atTop atTop

name: tendsto_nat_ceil_atTop
type_signature: {α : Type*} [Semiring α] [LinearOrder α] [IsStrictOrderedRing α] [FloorSemiring α] : Tendsto (fun x : α ↦ ⌈x⌉₊) atTop atTop

name: tendsto_nat_floor_mul_atTop
type_signature: {α : Type _} [Semifield α] [LinearOrder α] [IsStrictOrderedRing α] [FloorSemiring α] [Archimedean α] (a : α) (ha : 0 < a) : Tendsto (fun (x : ℕ) => ⌊a * x⌋₊) atTop atTop

name: tendsto_nat_floor_mul_div_atTop
type_signature: {a : R} (ha : 0 ≤ a) : Tendsto (fun x ↦ (⌊a * x⌋₊ : R) / x) atTop (𝓝 a)

name: tendsto_nat_floor_div_atTop
type_signature: : Tendsto (fun x ↦ (⌊x⌋₊ : R) / x) atTop (𝓝 1)

name: tendsto_nat_ceil_mul_div_atTop
type_signature: {a : R} (ha : 0 ≤ a) : Tendsto (fun x ↦ (⌈a * x⌉₊ : R) / x) atTop (𝓝 a)

name: tendsto_nat_ceil_div_atTop
type_signature: : Tendsto (fun x ↦ (⌈x⌉₊ : R) / x) atTop (𝓝 1)

name: Nat.tendsto_div_const_atTop
type_signature: {n : ℕ} (hn : n ≠ 0) : Tendsto (· / n) atTop atTop

name: IsUpperSet.thickening'

name: IsLowerSet.thickening'
type_signature: protected theorem IsLowerSet.thickening' (hs : IsLowerSet s) (ε : ℝ) : IsLowerSet (thickening ε s)

name: IsUpperSet.cthickening'
type_signature: protected theorem IsUpperSet.cthickening' (hs : IsUpperSet s) (ε : ℝ) : IsUpperSet (cthickening ε s)

name: IsLowerSet.cthickening'
type_signature: protected theorem IsLowerSet.cthickening' (hs : IsLowerSet s) (ε : ℝ) : IsLowerSet (cthickening ε s)

name: upperClosure_interior_subset'
type_signature: @[to_additive upperClosure_interior_subset] lemma upperClosure_interior_subset' (s : Set α) : (upperClosure (interior s) : Set α) ⊆ interior (upperClosure s)

name: lowerClosure_interior_subset'
type_signature: @[to_additive lowerClosure_interior_subset] lemma lowerClosure_interior_subset' (s : Set α) : (lowerClosure (interior s) : Set α) ⊆ interior (lowerClosure s)

name: IsUpperSet.mem_interior_of_forall_lt
type_signature: (hs : IsUpperSet s) (hx : x ∈ closure s) (h : ∀ i, x i < y i) : y ∈ interior s

name: IsLowerSet.mem_interior_of_forall_lt
type_signature: (hs : IsLowerSet s) (hx : x ∈ closure s) (h : ∀ i, y i < x i) : y ∈ interior s

name: dist_inf_sup_pi
type_signature: (x y : ι → ℝ) : dist (x ⊓ y) (x ⊔ y) = dist x y

name: dist_mono_left_pi
type_signature: : MonotoneOn (dist · y) (Ici y)

name: dist_mono_right_pi
type_signature: : MonotoneOn (dist x) (Ici x)

name: dist_anti_left_pi
type_signature: : AntitoneOn (dist · y) (Iic y)

name: dist_anti_right_pi
type_signature: : AntitoneOn (dist x) (Iic x)

name: dist_le_dist_of_le_pi
type_signature: (ha : a₂ ≤ a₁) (h₁ : a₁ ≤ b₁) (hb : b₁ ≤ b₂) : dist a₁ b₁ ≤ dist a₂ b₂

name: IsUpperSet.exists_subset_ball
type_signature: (hs : IsUpperSet s) (hx : x ∈ closure s) (hδ : 0 < δ) : ∃ y, closedBall y (δ / 4) ⊆ closedBall x δ ∧ closedBall y (δ / 4) ⊆ interior s

name: IsLowerSet.exists_subset_ball
type_signature: (hs : IsLowerSet s) (hx : x ∈ closure s) (hδ : 0 < δ) : ∃ y, closedBall y (δ / 4) ⊆ closedBall x δ ∧ closedBall y (δ / 4) ⊆ interior s

name: IsClosed.upperClosure_pi
type_signature: protected lemma IsClosed.upperClosure_pi (hs : IsClosed s) (hs' : BddBelow s) : IsClosed (upperClosure s : Set (ι → ℝ))

name: IsClosed.lowerClosure_pi
type_signature: protected lemma IsClosed.lowerClosure_pi (hs : IsClosed s) (hs' : BddAbove s) : IsClosed (lowerClosure s : Set (ι → ℝ))

name: IsClopen.upperClosure_pi
type_signature: protected lemma IsClopen.upperClosure_pi (hs : IsClopen s) (hs' : BddBelow s) : IsClopen (upperClosure s : Set (ι → ℝ))

name: IsClopen.lowerClosure_pi
type_signature: protected lemma IsClopen.lowerClosure_pi (hs : IsClopen s) (hs' : BddAbove s) : IsClopen (lowerClosure s : Set (ι → ℝ))

name: closure_upperClosure_comm_pi
type_signature: (hs : BddBelow s) : closure (upperClosure s : Set (ι → ℝ)) = upperClosure (closure s)

name: closure_lowerClosure_comm_pi
type_signature: (hs : BddAbove s) : closure (lowerClosure s : Set (ι → ℝ)) = lowerClosure (closure s)

name: isFixedPt_id
type_signature: (x : α) : IsFixedPt id x
docstring: Every point is a fixed point of `id`.

name: forall_isFixedPt_iff
type_signature: @[simp] theorem forall_isFixedPt_iff : (∀ x, IsFixedPt f x) ↔ f = id
docstring: A function fixes every point iff it is the identity.

name: eq
type_signature: protected theorem eq (hf : IsFixedPt f x) : f x = x
docstring: If `x` is a fixed point of `f`, then `f x = x`. This is useful, e.g., for `rw` or `simp`.

name: iterate
type_signature: protected theorem iterate (hf : IsFixedPt f x) (n : ℕ) : IsFixedPt f^[n] x
docstring: If `x` is a fixed point of `f`, then it is a fixed point of `f^[n]`.

name: left_of_comp
type_signature: (hfg : IsFixedPt (f ∘ g) x) (hg : IsFixedPt g x) : IsFixedPt f x
docstring: If `x` is a fixed point of `f ∘ g` and `g`, then it is a fixed point of `f`.

name: to_leftInverse
type_signature: (hf : IsFixedPt f x) (h : LeftInverse g f) : IsFixedPt g x
docstring: If `x` is a fixed point of `f` and `g` is a left inverse of `f`, then `x` is a fixed point of `g`.

name: map
type_signature: protected theorem map {x : α} (hx : IsFixedPt fa x) {g : α → β} (h : Semiconj g fa fb) : IsFixedPt fb (g x)
docstring: If `g` (semi)conjugates `fa` to `fb`, then it sends fixed points of `fa` to fixed points of `fb`.

name: apply
type_signature: protected theorem apply {x : α} (hx : IsFixedPt f x) : IsFixedPt f (f x)

name: preimage_iterate
type_signature: {s : Set α} (h : IsFixedPt (Set.preimage f) s) (n : ℕ) : IsFixedPt (Set.preimage f^[n]) s

name: image_iterate
type_signature: {s : Set α} (h : IsFixedPt (Set.image f) s) (n : ℕ) : IsFixedPt (Set.image f^[n]) s

name: equiv_symm
type_signature: protected theorem equiv_symm (h : IsFixedPt e x) : IsFixedPt e.symm x

name: perm_inv
type_signature: protected theorem perm_inv (h : IsFixedPt e x) : IsFixedPt (⇑e⁻¹) x

name: perm_pow
type_signature: protected theorem perm_pow (h : IsFixedPt e x) (n : ℕ) : IsFixedPt (⇑(e ^ n)) x

name: perm_zpow
type_signature: protected theorem perm_zpow (h : IsFixedPt e x) : ∀ n : ℤ, IsFixedPt (⇑(e ^ n)) x | Int.ofNat _ => h.perm_pow _ | Int.negSucc n => (h.perm_pow <| n + 1).perm_inv

name: Injective.isFixedPt_apply_iff
type_signature: (hf : Injective f) {x : α} : IsFixedPt f (f x) ↔ IsFixedPt f x

name: fixedPoints
type_signature: (f : α → α) : Set α
docstring: The set of fixed points of a map `f : α → α`.

name: mem_fixedPoints
type_signature: : x ∈ fixedPoints f ↔ IsFixedPt f x

name: mem_fixedPoints_iff
type_signature: {α : Type*} {f : α → α} {x : α} : x ∈ fixedPoints f ↔ f x = x

name: fixedPoints_id
type_signature: : fixedPoints (@id α) = Set.univ

name: fixedPoints_subset_range
type_signature: : fixedPoints f ⊆ Set.range f

name: Semiconj.mapsTo_fixedPoints
type_signature: {g : α → β} (h : Semiconj g fa fb) : Set.MapsTo g (fixedPoints fa) (fixedPoints fb)
docstring: If `g` semiconjugates `fa` to `fb`, then it sends fixed points of `fa` to fixed points of `fb`.

name: invOn_fixedPoints_comp
type_signature: (f : α → β) (g : β → α) : Set.InvOn f g (fixedPoints <| f ∘ g) (fixedPoints <| g ∘ f)
docstring: Any two maps `f : α → β` and `g : β → α` are inverse of each other on the sets of fixed points of `f ∘ g` and `g ∘ f`, respectively.

name: mapsTo_fixedPoints_comp
type_signature: (f : α → β) (g : β → α) : Set.MapsTo f (fixedPoints <| g ∘ f) (fixedPoints <| f ∘ g)
docstring: Any map `f` sends fixed points of `g ∘ f` to fixed points of `f ∘ g`.

name: bijOn_fixedPoints_comp
type_signature: (f : α → β) (g : β → α) : Set.BijOn g (fixedPoints <| f ∘ g) (fixedPoints <| g ∘ f)
docstring: Given two maps `f : α → β` and `g : β → α`, `g` is a bijective map between the fixed points of `f ∘ g` and the fixed points of `g ∘ f`. The inverse map is `f`, see `invOn_fixedPoints_comp`.

name: Commute.invOn_fixedPoints_comp
type_signature: (h : Commute f g) : Set.InvOn f g (fixedPoints <| f ∘ g) (fixedPoints <| f ∘ g)
docstring: If self-maps `f` and `g` commute, then they are inverse of each other on the set of fixed points of `f ∘ g`. This is a particular case of `Function.invOn_fixedPoints_comp`.

name: Commute.left_bijOn_fixedPoints_comp
type_signature: (h : Commute f g) : Set.BijOn f (fixedPoints <| f ∘ g) (fixedPoints <| f ∘ g)
docstring: If self-maps `f` and `g` commute, then `f` is bijective on the set of fixed points of `f ∘ g`. This is a particular case of `Function.bijOn_fixedPoints_comp`.

name: Commute.right_bijOn_fixedPoints_comp
type_signature: (h : Commute f g) : Set.BijOn g (fixedPoints <| f ∘ g) (fixedPoints <| f ∘ g)
docstring: If self-maps `f` and `g` commute, then `g` is bijective on the set of fixed points of `f ∘ g`. This is a particular case of `Function.bijOn_fixedPoints_comp`.

name: isFixedPt_of_tendsto_iterate
type_signature: {x y : α} (hy : Tendsto (fun n => f^[n] x) atTop (𝓝 y)) (hf : ContinuousAt f y) : IsFixedPt f y
docstring: If the iterates `f^[n] x` converge to `y` and `f` is continuous at `y`, then `y` is a fixed point for `f`.

name: isClosed_fixedPoints
type_signature: (hf : Continuous f) : IsClosed (fixedPoints f)
docstring: The set of fixed points of a continuous map is a closed set.
